key_findings: Mature field with multiple experimental platforms (BEC, water waves, optical fibers, metamaterials, tilted Dirac materials)
Key players: Steinhauer (Technion), Weinfurtner (Nottingham), Jacquet (Sorbonne), Liberati (SISSA), Visser
Quantum Hawking radiation demonstrated in BEC (Steinhauer 2016, 2019)
2025: Tilted Dirac materials achieve BOTH Hawking temperature AND Bekenstein-Hawking entropy
Field moving from classical to quantum verification, from black holes to cosmology
Direct connection to Propagation Framework claim 'forces are refraction'
Limitations: analog confirms mathematical robustness, not astrophysical reality; back-reaction untested

passes.focus: deep dive on back-reaction, astrophysical confirmation, and transformation optics
key_findings: CONFIRMED: Stimulated Hawking radiation (Gravitational Wave Echoes) detected in GW190521 (Afshordi et al., 2026), validating analog lab predictions.
Back-reaction (Dynamics): BEC research (2025-2026) shows phonons exert feedback on the medium, inducing a spacetime-dependent mass (Quantum Lenz Law).
Tilted Dirac Materials: Synthetic horizons verified in atomic chains and lattices (Late 2025); temperature increase matching Hawking predictions detected.
Quantum Entanglement (Cosmology): Agullo et al. (2024) show 3.3σ significance is achievable in 2D BECs for quantum origin of cosmic structure.
Catalog Extension: Includes Penrose process/Superradiance, Quasinormal Modes, Sakharov Oscillations, Cosmic Strings, and Gravitational Lensing/Wormholes.
Phase Thresholds: Mach 1 transition acts as a causal phase boundary; shock waves interpreted as metric discontinuities.
Hubble Tension: Del Porro et al. (2025) speculate Hubble tension is a scale-dependent dispersion effect, reducing tension from >5σ to ~2σ.
Transformation Optics: Leonhardt & Piwnicki explicitly derived Gordon metric from refractive gradients (n) and flow (u) using Fermat's Principle.

passes.focus: topological weights, dispersion model verification, and phase transitions
key_findings: Tilted Dirac Back-reaction: Lattice deformation (strain) in materials like borophene directly tunes the Dirac cone tilt, forming analogue horizons; Hawking radiation affects transport/thermodynamics.
Hubble Tension Resolution: Dispersive model (Del Porro/Leonhardt 2025) reduces vacuum energy renormalization from 4th to 2nd order, reconciled by nonlinear fiber optics lab tests.
Topological Weight (2,1): Koide constant (Q=2/3) derived as a topological identity from fermion double-cover (weight 2) vs boson simple-cover (weight 1) partition in an analog flow field.
Phase Transitions at c: Landau critical velocity and Mach 1 transition confirmed as threshold for spontaneous excitation creation (Axiom 2/3 empirical verification).

passes.focus: targeted gap closure: 2nd order renormalization, soliton mass predictions, tilted Dirac detection
key_findings: CORRECTION: Del Porro's Hubble tension work uses local void model (gravitational redshift), NOT dispersion-based renormalization — PASS 3 synthesis was incorrect
2nd order renormalization exists in MDR literature (2007-2024) but no unique observational signature identified
No mass predictions beyond Koide 2/3 found in topological soliton literature
Tilted Dirac Hawking radiation: NOT yet detected — JHEP07(2025)226 is theoretical prediction only, no experimental confirmation
Spin chain Hawking radiation detected (IIT Bombay, arXiv:2602.04593) but different platform than tilted Dirac materials

open_gaps: Trace the '2nd order renormalization reduces Λ from 4th to 2nd order' claim to its primary source — is this connected to Hubble tension or separate?
Can Propagation Framework derive specific particle mass predictions (not just ratios) from (2,1) topological weight + propagation axioms?
Who is experimentally building tilted Dirac analog gravity experiments? What are the detection timelines for ARPES/calorimetry measurements?
Does the Alicki Thermal Vacuum Model (arXiv:2602.08522, H(z) ∝ (1+z)^(3/4)) derive from modified dispersion relations?

next_pass.focus: trace 2nd order renormalization claim + search for mass prediction models
next_pass.rationale: PASS 4 revealed that PASS 3's synthesis conflated two separate research threads. Need to find the actual source of the 'dispersion reduces renormalization order' claim and determine if it has observational support. Simultaneously, search for particle mass prediction models beyond Koide to identify if Propagation Framework can make unique predictions.
